title: notifications-service failures: SMS provider 429s amplified by retry storm
error_signature: 429 Too Many Requests from sms-provider; retries amplifying load
summary: notifications-service began failing to send SMS around 12:30 UTC. sms-provider returns 429; notifications-service retries immediately without backoff, multiplying outbound volume and sustaining the throttling into a steady failure.

ground_truth.root_cause: sms-provider started rate-limiting (429) once notification volume crossed its account quota (100 req/s). notifications-service retries 429s immediately with no backoff, no jitter, and ignoring Retry-After, so each throttled request becomes 3-4 more requests (~4x amplification). This self-inflicted retry storm keeps the provider permanently over its limit. The trigger is the provider quota; the amplifier is the naive retry policy.
key_evidence: sms-provider log: '429 Too Many Requests: account quota 100 req/s exceeded (current 380 req/s)'
notifications-service log: 'sms-provider returned 429, retry 3/3 immediately (no backoff)'
notifications-service log: outbound rate 380 req/s vs inbound send rate 95 req/s (~4x amplification)
expected_fix_themes: add exponential backoff + jitter and honor the provider's Retry-After header
cap retries and shed/queue load (token-bucket rate limiter) to stay under the provider quota
add a circuit breaker so sustained 429s stop the retry storm; request a higher quota or batch sends
